description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular base constructed from interlocking gray and brown stone blocks. The well is topped with a wooden gabled roof supported by two vertical wooden posts, providing shelter from above. A hand crank mechanism is attached to one side, likely used for drawing water, and is made of dark wood or metal. The interior of the well is open and hollow, allowing access to the water below. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the stonework appears roughly hewn, giving it a rustic appearance. The overall design is practical, prioritizing function and durability for urban or park settings.
description: A circular stone water well with a wooden gabled roof and a hand crank mechanism, featuring rough stonework and a sturdy, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, and the hand crank mechanism on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden support post and the open circular interior of the well.
description_view_2: Rear view with the roof and stone wall visible, but the hand crank is less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the wooden post and the open well interior, with no crank visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled wooden roof supported by posts, no lid or slab covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Hand crank mechanism attached to the side, no visible bucket or pulley.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening.
